personality_one_liner: A sharp, OLED-friendly command center for shared household finances that prioritizes glanceable metrics, immediate hierarchy, and fast data entry.

spacing.rule: Dense and tactical. Use dividers and tonal separation instead of heavy shadows. Ensure generous touch targets (min 44pt) despite dense data.

do's: Sticky header backgrounds over scrolling content

screens.name: Onboarding
screens.purpose: Introduce the app and handle Google Sign-in for the household.

states.loading: Spinner in the center of the screen

screens.layout: Full-bleed background image with a vertical gradient scrim (transparent to surfaceInverse) at the bottom. The Google Sign-In button sits in the bottom 30% area, maintaining high contrast.

screens.name: Dashboard
screens.purpose: Provide a high-level view of household expenses across time periods, broken down by category.

states.loading: Skeleton loaders for the hero card and transaction list
states.empty: Illustration with 'No expenses logged yet' and a CTA to add one

screens.layout: Sticky top header with Period Selector chips (Daily/Weekly/Monthly/etc). Below that, a glowing Hero Card showing Total Expenses in INR (using Rajdhani font). Following is a grouped list (surfaceSecondary) showing category-wise expense breakdown with earner tags. A floating action button (FAB) for adding expenses is positioned at the bottom right.

screens.name: Categories

states.empty: Message 'No custom categories' with a 'Create Category' button

screens.layout: A structured list view. Each category item shows a tinted icon container (brandTertiary rounded square). Tapping opens a bottom sheet to edit or add subcategories. Sticky 'Add Category' CTA at the bottom.

screens.name: AddExpenseSheet
screens.purpose: Fast data entry for logging a new household expense with receipt attachment.

states.error: Inline validation errors for missing fields

screens.layout: A bottom sheet with a large numeric input for the INR amount at the top. Below are segmented controls/dropdowns for Category, Subcategory, and Paid-By (Earner selection). Includes a section for 'Attach Receipt' (camera/gallery icons) which shows a thumbnail preview when attached. Sticky 'Save Expense' button at the bottom.

screens.name: ProfileSettings
screens.purpose: Manage household members (earners) and app preferences.
screens.layout: A top hero section with the user's avatar. Below is a list of 'Household Members' showing avatars and roles. Includes buttons to 'Invite Member' and 'Log Out'. Uses divider-separated rows (surfaceSecondary) for settings options.

onboarding_background.alt: Dark abstract wavy obsidian and emerald background

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the Dark-First Utility (7) guidelines: use dividers and spacing instead of drop shadows. Keep the UI dense and highly scannable.
No blue or purple colors are permitted anywhere in the UI. Use the provided mint/emerald (brand) and zinc/gray (info) tones.
Implement the Dashboard with a sticky top period selector. Use the Rajdhani display font for all total numbers and large metrics to enhance the dashboard utility feel.
Add Expense must be a bottom sheet or a dedicated screen with a sticky bottom 'Save' CTA. Ensure the keyboard avoiding view is properly implemented so the button is always reachable.
Use real avatars for earners where possible, fallback to initials on a brandTertiary background.

Any image used as a background (e.g., Onboarding) MUST have a vertical gradient scrim (transparent to surfaceInverse or surface) to guarantee text contrast. Text should be placed in the darkest area of the scrim.
Include empty states for Dashboard and Categories using styled illustrations or icons, ensuring they fill the available space (flex: 1).
Ensure the INR symbol (₹) is correctly used alongside numbers.
Include 'react-native-image-picker' or 'expo-image-picker' implementation for the receipt attachment feature, handling both camera and gallery permissions.
Mock the Google Sign-in flow and CRUD backend operations if a real backend is not provided.
